summary: This page discusses converting complex numbers from Cartesian form to polar form, emphasizing the need to determine the correct quadrant when using the inverse tangent function. It introduces Example B.1 and works through the first two cases, showing how to compute magnitude and angle for numbers in the first and second quadrants, with Fig. B.4 illustrating the geometric interpretation.

figures.caption: Fig. B.4 From Cartesian to polar form.
